verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer in sentence 1: "Look at the actual SQL the endpoint runs"; A opens with diagnosis: "2M rows is small" before the concrete first move.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to one path: "Find the slow query first" then "Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE"; A gives an ordered checklist and a table of suspects, which feels more like a survey.
verdict.fabrication: A invents unsupported numbers: "single-digit milliseconds", "30-second check", "80% of the time"; B uses only the given 2M plus illustrative examples like "50k" and "OFFSET 100000".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it names the first artifact to obtain and tools: "pg_stat_statements", "slow query log", then "EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the real query with real parameter values".
